changes.summary: When configuration uses "*", refresh and read preserve the wildcard in state instead of replacing it with the API expanded permission list, reducing perpetual plan diffs.
When configuration uses "*" for a domain, refresh and read collapse multiple per-entity API policies back into a single wildcard policy to match configuration.
Updates no longer send default_role_id to the API; changing it in Terraform configuration will not apply to existing roles.
When linked to an integration credential and the API returns an empty secret, cascade update now restores the secret from the shared cache before updating the credential.
Creating or updating linked integration credentials now skips writing a nil client secret and logs a warning instead of failing.

Shared OAuth cache lookups now use clientId and clientSecret keys instead of client_id and client_secret; existing cache entries from earlier provider versions may not be found until secrets are refreshed.
Export now paginates through all web deployment configurations instead of returning only the first API page.
Determining the latest configuration version now paginates through all versions, improving accuracy for configurations with many versions.
Export now paginates through all web deployments instead of returning only the first API page.
Resolving the latest configuration version for a deployment now paginates through all versions, improving accuracy for configurations with many versions.
